tensions.actual: Technically complete: 30 CLI commands, 9 MCP tools, Stripe billing, magic link auth, cross-platform binaries, self-hosting. 0 paying users. Email sent to Stripe projects.dev for partner listing. No distribution strategy, no content, no demo video. Auth UX needs upgrade to browser-based device code flow. AGENTS.md stale.
tensions.desired: Have 10 paying users on spikes.sh

tensions.actual: Multiple half-finished features, outdated docs, 26 stale GitHub issues
tensions.desired: Product is clean, polished, and finished

tensions.actual: All 26 issues (10 spikes + 16 spikes-hosted) already closed
tensions.desired: All resolved GitHub issues are closed with commit references

tensions.actual: OVERHAUL.md and shaping.md deleted (git history preserves them)
tensions.desired: Outdated planning docs archived or deleted

tensions.actual: misc-2 and misc-post-release validation dirs deleted
tensions.desired: Validation reports reflect current state

tensions.actual: Removed phantom 'spikes dashboard' from llms-full.txt, fixed widget size (8KB→14KB) across all docs, corrected remote mode claim in agents.md
tensions.desired: Agent discovery pages accurate against shipped features

tensions.actual: MCP server is read-only, no write tools or remote mode
tensions.desired: Agents can act on feedback through MCP

tensions.actual: resolve_spike and delete_spike now work in both local and remote mode. PATCH/DELETE /spikes/:id endpoints added to hosted worker and self-hosting template.
tensions.desired: MCP write tools ship (submit, resolve, delete spikes)

tensions.actual: PATCH/DELETE endpoints added to hosted API. resolved/resolved_at columns added to schema with migration. MCP client wired up.
tensions.desired: MCP works against remote hosted backend

tensions.actual: Full onboarding flow verified and working end-to-end: local (init→inject→serve→feedback→list) and hosted (login via magic link email→share→share-link→shares). All technical blockers resolved. Magic link emails delivered via Resend from login@spikes.sh, verify landing page works, CLI poll detects verification. Only non-technical work remains: getting actual users.
tensions.desired: New user goes from discovery to first feedback to value with no confusion

tensions.actual: Fixed: removed phantom commands (dashboard), corrected CLI flags (list --type, export --output, inject flags), fixed env var names, updated MCP tool count and options, fixed install command
tensions.desired: Landing page and docs reflect only what's actually shipped

tensions.actual: Full flow verified end-to-end including hosted: init→inject→serve→feedback→list, login→share→share-link-live→shares-list all work. Fixed: port panic, auth.toml hints, README crate name, crates.io published (0.3.2), update command uses GitHub releases, D1 migrations applied (prod+staging), added GET /auth/poll to worker for CLI login, deployed. Remaining gap: magic link email not sent (no email service configured), workaround is --token flag.
tensions.desired: Install → first feedback → value moment is smooth and documented

tensions.actual: Device code flow works end-to-end but confirm step only asks for email without verifying it. Need: browser session cookie for instant confirm when already authenticated, email verification fallback for first-time users. Hybrid approach (option 3).
tensions.desired: Browser-based CLI auth flow (device code pattern)

tensions.actual: Submission prep shipped (commit 2c85bd7): packages/spikes-mcp/server.json manifest ready, mcpName added to NPM package, docs/mcp-directory-submissions.md has step-by-step for all 4 targets (MCP Registry, cursor.directory, Cursor marketplace, Windsurf). Actual submissions blocked on user GitHub device-code auth and outreach.
tensions.desired: Get listed in MCP directories (Claude, Cursor, Windsurf)

tensions.actual: Scaffolded: video/ Remotion project (React 18 + Remotion 4), full storyboard at video/STORYBOARD.md covering all 5 scenes, Title + Problem scenes implemented as working React components, CI-ready package.json + tsconfig, render/dev npm scripts, publish presets for web/YouTube/Twitter/GIF. Scenes 2-5 are storyboarded but not animated (est. 4-6h follow-up in Remotion Studio).
tensions.desired: Record 90-second demo video of full feedback loop using Remotion

tensions.actual: Intake form submitted 2026-04-13. Awaiting Stripe response (SLA: 2 business days). If admitted to May cohort, tensions #28 (marketing kit polish) + #29 (Stripe App build) + #30 (coordinated promo) activate in sequence. Repo URL fixes committed in cf0f7c0 + follow-up draft removed in 5ffd684.
tensions.desired: Stripe projects.dev partner listing

tensions.actual: Landing page now shows firm $49 lifetime Pro with LIFETIME badge, concrete tier features (widget/CLI/MCP on Free; unlimited + password + webhooks + API keys on Pro), and a Stripe buy button on the Pro card. Committed 07670e5.
tensions.desired: Sharpen pricing page — concrete tiers, clear value

tensions.actual: Full draft at posts/vibe-coding-feedback.md: 1,400-word primary post + 11-tweet Twitter variant + Show HN submission materials. Covers problem scene, format-not-capability thesis, structured packet example, end-to-end loop with MCP, honest rough edges, CTA. Ready to publish — awaiting venue choice.
tensions.desired: Write vibe-coding feedback post for distribution

tensions.actual: AGENTS.md references removed TUI, beads (not active), and lists shipped features as planned.
tensions.desired: Update AGENTS.md — remove stale references, add werk instructions

tensions.actual: Set up agent-ci, fixed 3 test failures and 1 clippy lint, all green
tensions.desired: CI passes locally via agent-ci (Docker runner)

tensions.actual: All 5 children resolved: auto-confirm removed, separate session token in both magic-link paths, CLI end-to-end verified against live worker, plaintext user_code dropped, stale site/auth/ cleaned up.
tensions.desired: Device code auth is phishing-resistant and end-to-end verified

tensions.actual: handleDevicePage has setTimeout(sessionConfirm, 500) — logged-in victims sent to attacker's code URL get auto-confirmed, handing attacker a bearer token
tensions.desired: Device confirm requires explicit user click, no auto-submit

tensions.actual: handleVerifyPage reuses CLI bearer token verbatim as spikes_session cookie; handleDeviceEmailVerify already does the right thing (mints separate token) — paths are inconsistent
tensions.desired: Session cookie uses a dedicated token separate from CLI bearer tokens

tensions.actual: CLI changes in cli/src/commands/login.rs are uncommitted, server is deployed but full loop never tested
tensions.desired: spikes login end-to-end flow verified against live worker

tensions.actual: user_code column stores plaintext but is never queried — only user_code_hash is used
tensions.desired: device_codes table stores only what it queries

tensions.actual: Untracked site/auth/device.html would be shadowed by the worker route if ever committed
tensions.desired: No dead /auth/device assets in spikes repo

tensions.actual: Form submitted at 2026-04-13T16:58:11Z. Google Forms confirmation received ('La tua risposta è stata registrata'). All 14 fields posted. Stripe commits to 2-business-day response per evaluation doc.
tensions.desired: Submit Stripe Projects intake form

tensions.actual: Draft complete at operations/stripe-projects-marketing-kit.md; needs logo export variants, /terms /privacy /support URL audit, co-marketing post approval workflow
tensions.desired: Marketing kit ready for Week 2/3 handoff

tensions.actual: Manifest + reverse APIs (provision, list-offerings, get-credentials, status, SPT) + new $9/mo Pro-Monthly SKU via SPT. Triggered only on May-cohort admission.
tensions.desired: Stripe App built + submitted (Week 2-4 if admitted)

tensions.actual: X posts coordinated with Stripe cohort announcement, demo video (#15) live, MCP Registry listing (#14) live, compound distribution hit in same window. Triggered only after Stripe publishes listing.
tensions.desired: May-cohort promotion executed (Week 5)

tensions.actual: inject has no endpoint option; scaffold README says 'add data-endpoint to your widget' but no CLI path to apply it to existing HTML — must hand-edit. Hit this 2026-04-22 wiring yvs backend at statecraft.systems/yvs/.
tensions.desired: spikes inject accepts --endpoint (and/or reads .spikes/config.toml [remote]) so widget wiring to a deployed Worker is one command

tensions.actual: widget.js silently sets postUrl=null and stores only to localStorage on HTTPS when no data-endpoint is set. Live on statecraft.systems/yvs/ for 2 days before I caught it; zero remote feedback collected even though the widget appeared to work. Real data loss.
tensions.desired: widget warns visibly (console.error + optional subtle UI badge) when it has no endpoint and is running on HTTPS — so developers notice their feedback is being silently dropped to localStorage

tensions.actual: spikes config prints 'Project: yvs' with defaults from any directory as if configured; spikes list from the same dir errors 'No .spikes/ directory found. Run spikes init first.' Inconsistent.
tensions.desired: spikes config from an uninitialized dir either says '(not initialized — run spikes init)' or exits non-zero; consistent with spikes list which errors out

tensions.actual: refuses 'Directory . already exists' even when empty. Surprising for a scaffold; forced me to cd out and pick a fresh subdir name.
tensions.desired: spikes deploy cloudflare --dir <path> accepts an existing empty directory (or --dir .)

tensions.actual: both paths exist for enabling remote feedback collection; unclear which is golden for 'static site already hosted elsewhere, just need a backend endpoint.' Had to read source + README to pick deploy cloudflare.
tensions.desired: docs include a one-paragraph decision guide for 'spikes share vs spikes deploy cloudflare' — when to use which

tensions.actual: exits 2 with 'No shares found' + Usage Summary on stderr on a clean account; looks like an error at first glance.
tensions.desired: spikes shares exits 0 on success (even when empty) and prints usage summary to stdout

tensions.actual: only per-project-Cloudflare-Worker path exists via 'spikes deploy cloudflare' — creates fresh Worker + D1 + token each time. For yvs this spun up a redundant Worker (spikes-worker-yvs.moritzbierling.workers.dev) parallel to the existing spikes.sh infrastructure. No multi-tenant 'add project to hosted service' flow surfaced in CLI.
tensions.desired: golden-path 'enable remote feedback for a static site I host elsewhere' is a single command: 'spikes project create <name>' on spikes.sh hosted backend, returns a ready-to-paste data-endpoint; deploy cloudflare is reframed as the self-host alternative

tensions.actual: 2026-04-22 ran the full 6-step README path (npm install → wrangler d1 create → paste id → wrangler d1 execute --remote --file=schema.sql → wrangler deploy). Worker live at https://spikes-worker-yvs.moritzbierling.workers.dev. D1 bound, token vars set. Only friction was wrangler d1 execute needing --remote flag (not spikes' fault).
tensions.desired: keep the spikes deploy cloudflare golden path as-is — it works end-to-end from scaffold to live Worker

tensions.actual: widget.js falls back to same-origin /spikes on HTTPS, which 404s silently on any site not hosted on spikes.sh. Forces every user to either set data-endpoint or stand up their own backend. The hosted multi-tenant backend at https://spikes.sh/spikes already exists and accepts POSTs with CORS *, but the widget doesn't use it by default. Discovered 2026-04-22 — I (creator) didn't know my own widget's default routing and scaffolded a redundant Worker.
tensions.desired: widget.js defaults to https://spikes.sh/spikes when data-project is set and no data-endpoint given — hosted path just works out of the box

tensions.actual: spikes deploy cloudflare is advertised as the golden path to 'add a backend', with no mention that spikes.sh is already a multi-tenant hosted backend. I (the creator of spikes.sh) used my own CLI to spin up a redundant per-project Worker + D1 for a 6-page demo site because the CLI never asked 'do you actually need your own backend?'. Two days of live widget running with no backend wiring is the dropoff signal.
tensions.desired: spikes deploy cloudflare prints a 'hold on — did you know spikes.sh hosts this for you?' prompt before scaffolding, OR the CLI has a separate spikes use-hosted command that just prints the widget tag with data-endpoint=https://spikes.sh/spikes pre-filled

tensions.actual: no mention of the hosted endpoint anywhere in README/docs/CLI. Had to probe with curl to discover POST https://spikes.sh/spikes works multi-tenant. And no discoverable read path — GET /spikes?project=yvs returns the marketing HTML, so there's no obvious way to view collected feedback after wiring the widget. Users will wire the widget, get feedback, and have no idea where to look.
tensions.desired: README, docs, and CLI output document https://spikes.sh/spikes as the canonical hosted endpoint, plus a one-line 'how to view your spikes for project=X' (dashboard URL or GET+token pattern)

tensions.actual: site/worker/ has README, package.json, tsconfig.json — but no index.ts, no wrangler.toml, no schema.sql. Looks like a stub. Makes the hosted backend opaque: from the public repo you cannot tell what fields the hosted /spikes endpoint accepts or what validation it runs. I discovered the real accepted shape by POSTing probes.
tensions.desired: site/worker/ in the repo contains the actual hosted Worker source (or a clear pointer to where it lives) so contributors can see what the hosted endpoint accepts

tensions.actual: hosted backend at POST https://spikes.sh/spikes is write-only from a user's perspective. All SPA routes (/dashboard, /p/yvs, /projects/yvs) serve the marketing HTML. spikes serve's dashboard only reads local file storage, not hosted D1. Spikes land in spikes-sh-db with no discoverable way for the project owner to view them. Without this, the hosted write path is pointless for anyone who isn't an admin with D1 query access. Blocking for the yvs → Tyler review loop: Tyler will click, feedback will land, Moritz cannot read it.
tensions.desired: hosted spikes.sh has a project-owner read surface: a dashboard or JSON endpoint at e.g. https://spikes.sh/p/<project>?token=<token> showing all spikes for that projectKey, with filters by page/rating/date

tensions.actual: nothing in CI catches the 'widget embedded on a customer site with no data-endpoint → silent 404' case. yvs ran for 2 days live with this exact failure and the test suite was green. A headless-browser test loading the widget on a file:// or third-party origin, doing a click-and-submit, and asserting a successful POST to a real endpoint would have caught it.
tensions.desired: CI smoke test fails the build if widget.js, loaded on an origin other than spikes.sh with data-project set and no data-endpoint, cannot reach a real backend

tensions.actual: POST /spikes accepts any projectKey from any origin with no auth. Anyone can submit spikes tagged 'yvs' or 'decideact-2026' and they will land in the owner's inbox once #43 ships a read surface. Asymmetric with the planned authed read path — write side needs a matching decision. Options: signed projectKey+secret pair, CORS allowlist per project, origin registration at project create time, or explicit 'anyone can write, owner moderates' posture (but then document that choice).
tensions.desired: hosted spikes.sh POST /spikes requires proof of project-key ownership; writes from an unauthorized origin to a projectKey owned by someone else are rejected

tensions.actual: shares.ts:431 unconditionally appends a widget <script> before </body>. Pages that self-instrument with the Spikes widget (e.g. yvs/*.html) render two stacked red FAB tiles on the share — one for the page's own data-project, one for the share id.
tensions.desired: spikes-hosted share worker injects widget.js idempotently — no duplicate widget when shared HTML already includes <script src=".../widget.js">

tensions.actual: Today the share-worker injects its own widget pointing at the share id, and the customer's self-hosted page points at their own data-project. Feedback is split across two project ids — and including both scripts produces duplicate FAB widgets. No mechanism to declare 'this share id and this customer project id are the same store'.
tensions.desired: Widget supports dual hosting: a page can be served both from a spikes.sh share and from the customer's own domain, with feedback from both surfaces flowing into the same project store

tensions.actual: Spikes works but provides no feedback
tensions.desired: We have explored, decided on, and implemented a bit of subtle feedback the user gets when they take actions with the widget
